name: Marathon County Employees
description_short: Marathon County Employees Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution in Wausau, WI offering comprehensive banking, lending, and financial planning services to its members.
description_long:
Marathon County Employees Credit Union (MCECU) is a credit union based in Wausau, Wisconsin that serves as a full-service financial institution for its membership. As a credit union, it operates on a cooperative model where members are owners rather than customers of a for-profit bank. The organization has been serving the Marathon County community and provides a range of traditional banking services alongside member-focused benefits.

MCECU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including multiple savings account types (regular savings, money market, Christmas club, health savings accounts, CDs), checking accounts tailored to different member segments (regular, 55+, student, and small business), and retirement accounts (Roth, SEP, regular, education IRAs). On the lending side, they provide auto loans for new and used vehicles, recreational vehicle loans, mortgages of all types, home equity loans, debt consolidation loans, lines of credit, signature loans, and credit cards. Members can access these products through multiple channels including online banking, a mobile app, drive-through services, and phone banking.

What distinguishes MCECU is its emphasis on member convenience and personalized service. The credit union operates a Lake View branch with extended hours (Tuesdays and Thursdays 11am-3pm), offers free notary services, provides on-site financial counselors, and maintains a surcharge-free ATM network accessible nationwide through shared branch systems. They emphasize quick loan approval turnaround, offer competitive rates (loans advertised as low as 3.74% APR), and provide value-added services like remote deposit capture, foreign currency exchange, and no-fee checking accounts across multiple member segments.

MCECU represents a traditional credit union model that prioritizes member relationships and community service over profit maximization. The organization is well-suited for individuals seeking relationship banking with competitive rates and personalized financial counseling. However, as a credit union with limited branch locations, members should verify membership eligibility requirements and confirm service availability for their specific needs.
services: Regular, student, 55+, and small business checking accounts (fee-free)
Savings accounts including money market, Christmas club, health savings, and certificates of deposit
Individual retirement accounts (Roth, conversion Roth, SEP, regular, education)
Auto loans for new and used vehicles with competitive rates
Mortgage loans and home equity loans
Debt consolidation loans and lines of credit
Remote deposit capture (RDC) via mobile app
Online bill pay and automatic payments/direct deposit
24-hour telephone teller ('MARS' member audio response system)
Mobile banking app and online banking portal
Surcharge-free ATM network access nationwide with dedicated locator
Financial planning and on-site financial counselor services
Free notary service and gift/travel cards
pros: Competitive loan rates starting as low as 3.74% APR with quick approval and turnaround times
Comprehensive product suite including savings accounts, checking, loans, mortgages, and retirement accounts
No-fee checking accounts available across multiple member segments (regular, 55+, student, small business)
Surcharge-free ATM access nationwide through shared branch network and dedicated ATM locator tool
Free member services including notary, financial counseling from on-site counselors, and remote deposit capture
Multiple account access channels: online banking, mobile app, drive-through, phone banking (24-hour telephone teller)
Value-added services like foreign currency exchange, automatic payments, direct deposit, and bill pay
cons: Limited physical branch presence (Lake View branch only open Tuesdays/Thursdays 11am-3pm) restricts in-person service access
Membership eligibility requirements not disclosed on website; unclear who qualifies to join the credit union
Limited information provided about loan rates for specific product types; only general promotional rate mentioned
Website does not specify dividend rates, account fees, or minimum balance requirements for any products
No mention of FDIC/NCUA insurance coverage, which is standard but should be clearly communicated
best_for: Marathon County area residents and employees seeking relationship-based banking with personalized service
Members qualifying for credit union membership who prioritize competitive rates and fee-free checking
Small business owners needing accessible business checking and lending products in the Wausau area
Individuals seeking comprehensive financial counseling and mortgage services from a locally-focused institution
diagnosis: MCECU is best for individuals with access to credit union membership who prefer relationship banking, competitive rates, and personalized financial counseling in the Wausau, Wisconsin area. The primary caveat is limited branch availability and unclear membership eligibility; prospective members should verify they qualify and confirm service accessibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Marathon County Employees — NCUA-insured credit union in WAUSAU, WI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
